title: ToolSandbox: A Stateful, Conversational, Interactive Evaluation Benchmark for LLM Tool Use Capabilities
authors: Jiarui Lu; Thomas Holleis; Yizhe Zhang; et al. (Ruoming Pang), Apple
publication_status: published (NAACL 2025 Findings; DBLP confirmed; anthology page fetch failed, exact anthology ID unverified)
tables_figures_checked: Sec.1-5 + App.A via ar5iv: Table 1 (benchmark comparison), Table 2 (user simulator ablation), Table 3 (statistics), Table 4 (main results 13 models), Fig.1-3, Eq.1 (milestone/minefield scoring)
overlap_reason: Supplies the world-state/state-dependency semantics and outcome-level milestone evaluation that C3 needs as backdrop, but is a benchmark with no reuse/verification/rollback mechanism.
unresolved_questions: Exact ACL Anthology ID unverified (network failure). Task-list title differs slightly from actual title. v1 author 'Felix Bai' vs NAACL 'Haoping Bai' (same person renamed?) unconfirmed.

title: Fast Inference from Transformers via Speculative Decoding
authors: Yaniv Leviathan; Matan Kalman; Yossi Matias (Google)
venue: ICML 2023 (Oral; PMLR pp. 19274-19286 per citing sources)
full_text_obtained: partial (Sec.1-3.5 read via ar5iv; Sec.4 empirical tables not in fetched excerpt; 2X-3X T5-XXL figure cross-confirmed by abstract+intro)
tables_figures_checked: Alg.1, Fig.1-2, Thm 3.5/3.8, Sec.3 analysis; Sec.4 walltime tables not directly inspected
overlap_reason: Paradigm ancestor of speculate-verify-recommit, but token-level, stateless, distribution-exact verification; no external state, no side effects, no checkpoints - defines the boundary C3 contrasts with.
unresolved_questions: No official code artifact. Exact table numbers for Sec.4 walltime results not verified.

title: A Plan Reuse Mechanism for LLM-Driven Agent (AgentReuse)
authors: Guopeng Li; Ruiqi Wu; Haisheng Tan (USTC)
venue: Journal of Computer Research and Development (2024, Chinese); arXiv English version 2025
tables_figures_checked: Sec.1-6.3 via HTML v2: Alg.1, Fig.1-4, Table 1 (similarity eval F1 0.9718 / acc 0.9459); latency tables after Sec.6.3 not fully fetched
overlap_reason: Occupies 'reuse historical agent artifacts for latency' but validity is judged BEFORE reuse (intent classification + similarity threshold); no post-hoc verification, no rollback; reuses plan templates, not intermediate computation - the pre-use-judgment paradigm C3 explicitly contrasts against.
unresolved_questions: Internal inconsistency: abstract claims 93.12% latency reduction vs Sec.6 text '60.61 percentage points' (different baselines). No public code. gamma=0.75 threshold is empirical.

title: ChainCaps: Composition-Safe Tool-Using Agents via Monotonic Capability Attenuation
authors: Xiaochong Jiang; Shiqi Yang; Ziwei Li; et al. (6 authors)
venue: 2nd Workshop on Agents in the Wild (AIWILD) at ICML 2026
tables_figures_checked: Full text via HTML v3: Fig.1-3, Table 1 (live ASR 25-68%->0-4.8%, benign 96-100%), Table 2 (replay vs Fides/PFI), Table 3 (ablation + manifest quality), Thm 3.1, Prop 3.2
overlap_reason: Runtime invariant enforcement over composed tool chains, but the invariant is authority attenuation (security), not staleness/validity of reused computation; preventive blocking, no reuse/verification/rollback.
unresolved_questions: 'Code and artifact availability' section is EMPTY in the paper - claimed ~1200-line MCP proxy has no public artifact; all experimental claims unverifiable by artifact. Residual shell-pipe laundering failures (8% ASR).

title: Information Fidelity in Tool-Using LLM Agents: A Martingale Analysis of the Model Context Protocol
authors: Flint Xiaofeng Fan; Cheston Tan; Roger Wattenhofer; Yew-Soon Ong
venue: AAMAS 2026 (extended abstract; full version on arXiv)
publication_status: accepted extended abstract (AAMAS 2026, per arXiv comment, v1 2026-02-10)
tables_figures_checked: Main body via HTML v1: Eq.1, Def.1-6, Lemma 1, Thm 1, Cor.1-2, Fig.1-3, Sec.5 baseline validation (Qwen2-7B D(10)=5.26+-0.34 vs envelope 9.15; Llama-3-8B 4.92+-0.46 vs 8.90; beta_hat in [0.68,0.71])
overlap_reason: Theory of error accumulation across sequential tool calls (linear growth, O(sqrt(T)) deviation, ~9-step re-grounding); re-grounding is periodic refresh - conceptually opposite to reusing stale computation; no reuse/verifier-runtime/rollback.
unresolved_questions: Experiments use deterministic cached tools and beta is set by the query generator (controlled, not real-agent). Code repo exists but directory contents not inspected (page render failure) - implementation match unconfirmed.

title: Sherlock: Reliable and Efficient Agentic Workflow Execution
authors: Yeonju Ro; Haoran Qiu; Inigo Goiri; et al. (Esha Choukse), Microsoft Azure Research + UT Austin
venue: Under review at MLSys (per paper footnote)
tables_figures_checked: Full text: Fig.1-15, Table 1-3, Alg.1, Eq.1-8; Sec.7 in full (speculation depth bound Eq.4/8, cost model Eq.6, selective rollback Sec.7.2 ROUGE-L gate); Sec.8 eval (Table 3: LiveCodeBench T_exec -62.9%, T_vrf -48.7%; abstract: +18.3% acc, -26.0% cost vs AFlow)
overlap_reason: Implements the exact runtime skeleton of C3: optimistically proceed on unverified intermediate output + background outcome-level verifier + similarity-gated selective rollback + re-execution. Residual difference: speculates FRESH downstream computation on current unverified output (failure source = LLM error), not reuse of historical/stale intermediate computation after external state change (failure source = state drift); side-effect reversibility not addressed.
unresolved_questions: Under review - publication status may change. No public code. Selective rollback collapses to full rollback for code/math (similarity metrics AUC~0.5). Whether/how it handles irreversible side effects is not discussed in the paper.

title: Speculative Actions: A Lossless Framework for Faster Agentic Systems
authors: Naimeng Ye; Arnav Ahuja; Georgios Liargkovas; Yunan Lu; Kostis Kaffes; Tianyi Peng (Columbia University)
venue: ICLR 2026 Oral (per author homepages + OpenReview PDF; official proceedings page not re-confirmed)
openreview_id: exists (OpenReview PDF fetched; forum id not captured)
full_text_obtained: partial (abs v2 + HTML Sec.1 + GitHub README; body sections not read individually)
tables_figures_checked: Abstract figures (up to 55% next-action prediction accuracy, up to 20% latency reduction); Actor/Speculator design from Sec.1; cost-latency theory section not verified in detail
overlap_reason: The canonical representative of the opposite pole in C3's core question: predict FUTURE actions with a fast Speculator, pre-execute in parallel, commit only on exact match against the authoritative Actor's ground truth, discard/overwrite on mismatch. No stale/historical reuse; verification awaits ground truth rather than being a cheap outcome-level oracle.
unresolved_questions: ICLR 2026 Oral claim from author homepages, not official proceedings. Lossy extension (OS tuning, last-write-wins) has no general correctness guarantee. Table/figure numbers for the 55%/20% results not verified.

title: Dynamic Speculative Agent Planning (DSP)
authors: Yilin Guan; Qingfeng Lan; Sun Fei; Dujian Ding; Devang Acharya; Chi Wang; William Yang Wang; Wenyue Hua
full_text_obtained: partial (abs v3 + GitHub README; mechanism details partly second-hand via C3-P10 Sec.11 contrast table)
tables_figures_checked: Abstract numbers (cost -30%, unnecessary cost -60%, comparable efficiency to fastest lossless method); repo shows OpenAGI + TravelPlanner experiments, fix/dynamic k modes, tau/offset knobs
overlap_reason: Speculative-execution family (forward plan speculation with approximation agent + target agent verification, dynamic k via online RL); no historical/stale reuse, no post-hoc outcome verification with rollback.
unresolved_questions: Body not read section-by-section; C3-P10 claims DSP's training loss uses token counts not dollar rates (second-hand). Publication status: arXiv-only as of 2026-07.

title: Parallelizing Tool Execution and LLM Generation for Low-Latency Agent Serving (PASTE; v1 title: Act While Thinking)
authors: Yifan Sui; Han Zhao; Rui Ma; et al. (9 authors)
full_text_obtained: partial (abs v1/v3 + Sec.1 fragment + third-party GitHub issue summary; body not read)
tables_figures_checked: v3 abstract numbers (avg task completion time -43.5%, observed tool latency 1.8x lower); v1 abstract claimed -48.5% / 1.8x throughput (version discrepancy)
overlap_reason: Reasoning-time speculative tool pre-execution from mined call patterns, with isolation of speculative results until LLM confirmation; forward speculation, no historical reuse, no checkpoint rollback (speculative results never take effect before confirmation).
unresolved_questions: Retitled between v1 and v3 with changed headline numbers (48.5%->43.5%) - cite version carefully. No code found. Policy-compliance admission rule for speculative actions not verified in original text.

title: Cost-Aware Speculative Execution for LLM-Agent Workflows: An Integrated Five-Dimension Method
full_text_obtained: true (Sec.1-12.2 read via HTML v1; appendices and Sec.12.3+ not fully read)
tables_figures_checked: D1-D5 design, EV rule (Sec.6), admissibility precondition (Sec.3.3), Beta-Binomial posterior (Sec.7.3), k_crit numeric table (Sec.7.6), worked examples (Sec.10), prior-art contrast tables (Sec.11); validation is a synthetic suite (App.D) - no real-system experiments
overlap_reason: Decision-theoretic layer over the same forward-speculation paradigm. Directly relevant to C3 in two ways: (a) its side-effect admissibility triage (side-effect-free / idempotent / commit-barrier) is the constraint surface for C3's rollback safety; (b) its taxonomy explicitly names Sherlock's pattern ('post-output, pre-verification speculation') and separates it from pre-completion speculation - evidence the field is actively self-segmenting around C3's distinction.
unresolved_questions: Single-author arXiv paper; validation entirely synthetic; Sec.11 contrast table is self-reported and not third-party verified; predictor overhead acknowledged as possibly erasing gains (Sec.14.2).
